rulings.Case Title: DANIELLE PITCHER VS. LIZA BUCHBINDER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 18, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT U.S. BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend. See CCP §1061. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action for breach of Contract is overruled. Paragraphs 20-21 of the Complaint allege that Defendants breached the contract by failing to cooperate with the Section 8 housing scheme. While Plaintiff¿s allegations regarding the uninhabitable conditions are conclusory, the cause of action is still viable. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for breach of implied covenant is overruled. Paragraph 32 sufficiently alleges Defendant¿s breaches. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 4th cause of action for UCL is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not allege facts showing an entitlement to restitution or an injunction. See Madrid v. Perot Systems Corp., (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 440, 452. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 5th cause of action for wrongful eviction is overruled. Plaintiff does not plead facts as to how Defendant participated in the actual eviction of Plaintiff. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 6th cause of action is overruled. A claim under the San Francisco Administrative Code is viable merely if a Landlord wrongfully endeavors to recover possession. An actual eviction is unnecessary. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 7th cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to support this cause of action. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 8th cause of action for equitable estoppel is sustained without leave to amend. This is a defense. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 18, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT MOHAMMAD CHAHINE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The allegations of the FAC and the original Complaint do not directly conflict regarding defendants' being Board members or designated by their companies to sit on the Board. While ultimately Plaintiff will need to prove that these defendant's acted in their individual capacity and not as simply agents of their company in order for individual liability to attach, this issue does not need to be determined on a Demurrer. Moreover, because plaintiff seeks to hold defendants liable for their individual breaches as Board members, it is not necessary that all Board Members be joined in this action. Therefore the Demurrers are overruled on these grounds. Defendant Hudson also argues that Plaintiff's breach of fiduciary duty claim fails because all of the alleged Board breaches were duties were delegated. However, nowhere in the FAC does Plaintiff plead that the Board properly delegated each of the duties it purportedly breached. Therefore, this is a question of fact not properly addressed through a demurrer. As to the application of the Business Judgment Rule, this is an affirmative defense that can be raised in the answer. Plaintiff has pled fact sufficient to show that it may not apply in this instance. Accordingly the Demurrer is overruled on this ground as well. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Demurrers Filed By Ronald Hudson And Superior Wall System, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 18, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT MARTIN BROS./MARCOWALL, INC., A CALIFORNIA, MOHAMMAD CHAHINE Joinder In Demurrers Filed By Ronald Hudson And Superior Wall System, Inc. Defendant Superior Wall Systems Inc.'s Demurrer - Defendants' Demurrer to the 2nd, 6th and 7th causes of action is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to support its claims and may bring an action for Unjust enrichment. Furthermore, Defendants' Demurrer on the grounds that Plaintiff has failed to join an indispensible party or parties is overruled. Where there is joint and severable liability in contract, the Plaintiff is not required to join all of the parties to the action. Defendant Hudson's Demurrer - The allegations of the FAC and the original Complaint do not directly conflict regarding defendants' being Board members or designated by their companies to sit on the Board. While ultimately Plaintiff will need to prove that these defendant's acted in their individual capacity and not as simply agents of their company in order for individual liability to attach, this issue does not need to be determined on a Demurrer. Moreover, because plaintiff seeks to hold defendants liable for their individual breaches as Board members, it is not necessary that all Board Members be joined in this action. Therefore the Demurrers are overruled on these grounds. Defendant Hudson also argues that Plaintiff's breach of fiduciary duty claim fails because all of the alleged Board breaches were duties were delegated. However, nowhere in the FAC does Plaintiff plead that the Board properly delegated each of the duties it purportedly breached. Therefore, this is a question of fact not properly addressed through a demurrer. As to the application of the Business Judgment Rule, this is an affirmative defense that can be raised in the answer. Plaintiff has pled fact sufficient to show that it may not apply in this instance. Accordingly the Demurrer is overruled on this

rulings.Case Title: N & S NEUROLOGY CENTER, A MEDICAL GROUP, INC., A VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 18, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND, A PUBLIC ENTITY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS TO THE GROUNDS OF RES JUDICATA. HOWEVER THE DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD MORE FACTS TO SUPPORT ITS ALLEGATION THAT IT "ADVOCATED" FOR APPROPRIATE HEALTH CARE WHICH LED TO RETALIATORY ACTION. HOWEVER, IF THIS CANNOT BE PLED BECAUSE THE SUBJECT CASES ARE PENDING WITH THE WCAB, THEN THIS CASE IS NOT RIPE. LIKEWISE THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND ON THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION AS THAT IS STILL ON APPEAL AND IT WOULD BE IMPROPER FOR THIS COURT TO RULE ON THIS MATTER AT THIS TIME. =(302/LMG)
